setroubleshootd_t.note: setroubleshootd (SELinux troubleshooter daemon) was attempting to access RPM database files it is not allowed to write. These were pre-existing denials from before semodule -DB (dontaudit silenced them normally).

systemd_user_runtimedir_t.note: systemd-user-runtime-dir attempting net_admin capability (CAP_NET_ADMIN=12). Triggered on each SSH session login. Likely setting up per-user runtime directory network namespacing.

sshd_session_t.audit2allow_N: allow sshd_session_t chkpwd_t:process { noatsecure rlimitinh siginh };
sshd_session_t.note: sshd_session_t (new in el10 - sshd-session split from sshd) does not have full process privilege inheritance rights over chkpwd_t. This is a known policy gap in selinux-policy-42 for the new sshd-session binary introduced in OpenSSH 9.x.
sshd_session_t.permissive_note: Some of these were permissive=1 (in the semanage permissive list: sshd_session_t is a builtin permissive type)

sshd_t.note: sshd_t (main SSH daemon) cannot inherit signal disposition into sshd_session_t (new separate domain). Policy gap for the OpenSSH session-separation introduced in el10.

init_t.audit2allow_N: allow init_t chkpwd_t:process siginh; allow init_t unconfined_t:process siginh;
init_t.note: systemd (init_t) cannot inherit signal disposition into chkpwd_t or unconfined_t. PAM authentication path via unix_chkpwd triggers this. Normal for the init_t -> child domain transition.

logrotate_t.note: logrotate attempting to read a file mislabeled as shadow_t (actual file was /tmp/r10-s1-readonly-5486 which had been chcon'd or naturally labeled shadow_t). This was from the S1 measurement setup where the runner user created a file in /tmp that got shadow_t label.

el10_specific_findings: sshd_session_t is a NEW domain in el10 (OpenSSH 9.x session-process separation). Does not exist on el8/el9. Policy gap with sshd_t and init_t siginh is an el10-specific finding.
systemd_user_runtimedir_t net_admin denial appears on every SSH session login - el10-specific policy gap.
selinux-policy-42 has 21 builtin permissive types (vs fewer on el8/el9), reflecting newer system components.
audit 4.0.3 AVC record format is IDENTICAL to audit 3.x - no new fields. The 'ausearch version 4.0.3' note is the only visible change.
